nodes.label: Dev Jar Upload Workflow (build, verify datagen, SCP to VPS, notify bot)
Java CI Workflow (Gradle build on main PRs/pushes)
Crowdin Check & Upload Workflow (lang lint + source upload)
Release Prepare Workflow (stable-only manifest build + bot notify)
Release Publish Workflow (approved release to Modrinth/CurseForge)
DMZ Discord Release Bot (webhook on VPS, gates release approval and dev-jar publication)
Operating Rules for AI Agents
nodes.rationale: Working tree is shared; preserve user-created content; backward compatibility is not assumed unless save data, player-editable JSON, configs, or addons are affected; make the smallest coherent change.

nodes.label: DragonMineZ-Specific Agent Rules
nodes.rationale: Registry names must stay stable once exposed to worlds/saves/addons; packets append-only; never trust client input for progression; storage changes are high-risk for existing worlds.

nodes.label: Git Safety Rules
nodes.rationale: No destructive git operations; stage changed tracked files before finishing; never commit or amend unless requested.

nodes.label: Mod Boot Path (DragonMineZ entry class)
nodes.rationale: Entry constructor enforces the hard-incompatibility list (Legendary Tooltips, Epic Fight, Better Combat) before any init to fail fast with a clear IllegalStateException.

nodes.label: StatsData Player Capability
nodes.rationale: The 13 NBT keys written by save() are the save-format contract for all three storage backends; renaming any silently orphans existing NBT/JSON/DB data. load() hard-throws without PlayerQuestData, so it is a full-document loader, not a safe partial merge.

nodes.label: Packet Registration Order Defines Wire IDs
nodes.rationale: Packet ids come from a sequential counter, so registration order is the wire protocol; new packets must be appended, never inserted mid-list, or client/server builds desync.

nodes.label: Semver Config Versioning
nodes.rationale: Version is a semver string because legacy numeric versions collide as doubles (21.10 == 21.1); outdated/corrupt defaults are moved to oldBackup and regenerated rather than overwritten.

nodes.label: Three-Way Config Merge Against previousConfigs Baselines
nodes.rationale: Diffing user config against the previous release's bundled defaults lets balance changes propagate when the user kept defaults while preserving deliberate user edits; skipping the per-release baseline copy silently weakens the merge.

nodes.label: Quest Registry World-Save Loading
nodes.rationale: Existing world quest files are never overwritten by defaults, so changing default quest content in code has no effect on existing worlds unless files are deleted; this protects server-owner customizations.

nodes.label: Quest Reward Difficulty Allow-Set
nodes.rationale: Each reward carries an EnumSet of difficulties as an explicit allow-set (not a minimum threshold) so combinations like [EASY,HARD] are expressible; the old hard:/normal: type-string prefix was removed.

nodes.label: Wish and Dragonball Pipelines
nodes.rationale: There is no in-jar datapack wish JSON: reload listeners ignore scanned datapack JSON and delegate to DragonBallPackManager.loadAll; addon packs cannot introduce new blocks at runtime because RegistryObject refs are copied from bootstrap only.

nodes.label: Storage Backends (NBT/JSON/DATABASE)
nodes.rationale: Vanilla .dat capability persistence is always on; JSON/DATABASE double-persist on top with async login load overwriting vanilla values. On DB failure there is no fallback switch - durability comes only from the vanilla path.

nodes.label: Commands and DMZPermissions
nodes.rationale: hasPermission() contains a hard-coded name-based admin bypass for three developer usernames - flagged as a pattern not to replicate.

nodes.label: Beta Access Verification Flow
nodes.rationale: The dragonminez-ai bot flow is intentionally a narrow verification bridge (Discord OAuth to whitelist PR auto-approval), not a general public API.

nodes.label: CI and Release Automation Workflows
nodes.rationale: Jar SHA-256 mismatch on publish is a warning, not a failure, because resource optimization is not byte-reproducible; the Discord bot never holds Modrinth/CurseForge tokens - those stay in GitHub Actions secrets.

nodes.label: Player-Editable JSON Is A Compatibility Boundary
nodes.rationale: Many DragonMineZ JSON files are intended to be edited by players and addon authors, so readability, lenient loading, useful errors, and migration paths must be preserved.

nodes.label: Prefer Replacing Old Code Over Compatibility Branching
nodes.rationale: When backward compatibility was not requested, edit old code into the new behavior instead of layering parallel legacy paths.

nodes.label: Contiguous Kill Objectives Track Together
nodes.rationale: Quest-spawned enemies for all kill objectives spawn when the quest starts, so contiguous kill objectives must be treated as one tracking block rather than gating each behind earlier kill progress.

nodes.label: AI Doc Layering Model (Agents/Skills/Context/Memory)
nodes.rationale: Repeatable instructions are packaged into the right layer: onboarding rules in Agents.md, architecture in Context.md, durable lessons in Memory.md, procedural workflows in Skills.md - preferring repeatable workflows over one-off explanations.

nodes.label: Verification Workflow (Evidence Before Completion)
nodes.rationale: Never claim fixed/complete/passing without verification evidence: gradlew build for code, runData for generated data, runClient/runServer for gameplay, check_lang.sh for localization.

nodes.label: DragonMineZ Forge Mod
nodes.rationale: Minecraft Forge 1.20.1 mod (Java 17, mod id dragonminez) layering a Dragon Ball progression system over survival Minecraft; mandatory deps Forge, GeckoLib, TerraBlender, Curios; hard-incompatible with Legendary Tooltips, Epic Fight, Better Combat.

nodes.label: High-Risk Runtime Data Systems
nodes.rationale: The repo is data-heavy and player-customizable: config, quest, wish, and storage JSON is loaded at runtime from config folders, world saves, and addon packs, and must never be treated as internal-only or rewritten destructively.

nodes.label: Working Agreements
nodes.rationale: Smallest coherent change; backward compatibility only assumed for save data, player-editable JSON, configs, and addons; avoid destructive git operations; do not edit build/, run/, run-data/.

nodes.label: Defense System Rework (Flat Mitigation Units)
nodes.rationale: Defense stored as flat mitigation units so the displayed value directly represents damage reduction per hit; existing worlds auto-migrate at config 21.2 by multiplying defenseScaling by 0.12; the old transform DEF divider was removed in favor of higher per-form defMultiplier values.

nodes.label: Adaptive Defense Mitigation
nodes.rationale: A final proportional reduction layer strongest at damage-to-defense parity, decaying as damage exceeds defense (default cap 70%); hits are fully negated when flat mitigation reaches 3x incoming damage.

nodes.label: Quest Rewards Per Difficulty (JSON difficulty field)
nodes.rationale: Shipped as a developer/operator framework feature not applied to default quests until v2.2, letting addon authors gate rewards by difficulty ahead of official content.

nodes.label: Quest-Tunable Transformation Stats (Transform* fields)
nodes.rationale: Absolute overrides take precedence over multipliers; a transformDefaults block in entities.json sets global defaults that per-quest overrides always win over.

nodes.label: Form Skin Tint Config (tintColor/tintIntensity)
nodes.rationale: Replaces the old hardcoded Kaioken-only red tint with a generic per-form JSON config; user-defined form files are automatically version-migrated on load.

nodes.label: Structure Spawn Planning Overhaul (dragonminez_structure_plan)
nodes.rationale: Structure positions are resolved once and saved to world data so later starts skip the biome search; the overworld resolves synchronously at first world creation so near-spawn structures exist before the first spawn chunk generates.

nodes.label: TP Multiplier Additive Combination Fix
nodes.rationale: All TP bonuses are now combined additively (total = 1 + sum(bonus - 1)) because multiplicative stacking of several sources compounded and overstated TP gains; the HTC default multiplier was reduced from 2.5x to 1.75x as part of the same rebalance.

nodes.label: Smart Config Regeneration (previousConfigs snapshots)
nodes.rationale: Baseline config snapshots shipped in the jar let migration diff and apply only what changed, preserving server/user customizations that previous migrations could discard in edge cases.

nodes.label: Radial X Utility Menu Rework
nodes.rationale: Utility menu reorganized as a radial wheel with grouped form nodes and an addon API for registering custom buttons, reducing clutter for races with large transformation pools.

nodes.label: MobBattlePowerHelper (Accurate Mob BP Formula)
nodes.rationale: Non-DMZ mobs get BP computed from real entity attributes instead of the naive health + attack*5 estimate that grossly underestimated modded enemies; skipped for DMZ-managed entities and disableable via accurateMobBattlePower.

nodes.label: Patch Notes v2.1 (End of Z Intro)
Combo Melee Combat System
nodes.rationale: Replaces the old 1.8-combat timing gimmick with a fully animation-driven melee system: combo chains, data-driven weapon attributes across 31 weapon types, dual wielding, crits, block/parry/perfect evasion.

nodes.label: Beam Clash QTE Minigame
nodes.rationale: Clash outcome blends timing with stat power so stronger fighters start ahead but timing can decide close clashes.

nodes.label: DBSagas Story Mode and Quest Tree
Alignment System (Good/Neutral/Evil)
nodes.rationale: Masters Enma, Baba, and Toribot ignore alignment rules so core progression is never blocked; rules live in server-editable world-save JSON (alignment_rules.json).

nodes.label: RPG Classes and Passives
nodes.rationale: Paladin passive range capped at 50 blocks to prevent map-wide party lifesteal/damage-redirect effects.

nodes.label: Datapack-Driven Dragon Balls, Radars and Wishes
nodes.rationale: Dragon Ball, radar, and dragon entity systems merged into one extensible datapackable format so packs/addons can add or override ball sets, radars, dragons, and wishes.

nodes.label: Space Pod Destination System
nodes.rationale: Destinations are datapack-driven with composable unlock rules (AND/OR/NOT over quest completion, level, stats, race, tags, visited dimensions).

nodes.label: Curios API Required Dependency
nodes.rationale: Curios became mandatory in 2.1 because it powers Head Tech, Weights, and accessory slots.

nodes.label: Saga Difficulty Tiers (Easy/Normal/Hard)
nodes.rationale: Expanded from a single Hard Mode toggle to three tiers each tracking independent quest progress so players can replay sagas without losing completion records; party leaders control group difficulty.

nodes.label: Gravity Device Training Block
nodes.rationale: Enforces a hard stop threshold capping effective gravity at a configurable maximum to prevent runaway training conditions; consumes Star Energy from cables.

nodes.label: Crowdin Localization Config
nodes.rationale: Maps en_us.json as the single Crowdin source locale to 33 target locale filenames using Minecraft's locale_with_underscore naming.

nodes.label: Empty Vanilla Splashes Override
nodes.rationale: An empty splashes.txt shipped in the minecraft asset namespace overrides vanilla title-screen splash texts as part of the mod's custom title branding.

hyperedges.label: DragonMineZ Release Automation Pipeline (build, approve via Discord bot, publish)
